headings.text: From draft to dispatch in three steps
Ready to ship words at code-speed?

paragraphs.text: Luminos plugs into the tools your product team already lives in. Connect once, then turn raw tickets and Notion scratchpads into release-ready copy without leaving your flow.
OAuth into Notion, Jira, Linear, or GitHub in under 30 seconds. Read-only by default — Luminos never edits source documents.
Choose a single Jira epic, a Notion page, or batch-select a sprint's worth of issues. Pick your output: release note, changelog, or email.
Tune the tone, edit inline, and push straight to Confluence, Intercom, or your changelog page. Drafts stay versioned forever.
Search now feels instant. We've rebuilt the input layer to be smarter about when it queries, so results land the moment you stop typing.
Rolling out gradually this week — no action needed.
Join product teams at Stackwire, Driftlane, and Covalent HQ who've replaced "release-notes Friday" with a single keyboard shortcut.
The AI writing copilot for product teams. Polished release notes, changelogs, and customer emails — from the docs and tickets you already have.

visibleText: Luminos Hero Features How it works Pricing Early access Get access How it works From draft to dispatch in three steps Luminos plugs into the tools your product team already lives in. Connect once, then turn raw tickets and Notion scratchpads into release-ready copy without leaving your flow. 1 Connect your workspace OAuth into Notion, Jira, Linear, or GitHub in under 30 seconds. Read-only by default — Luminos never edits source documents. ~30 sec 2 Pick a doc or ticket Choose a single Jira epic, a Notion page, or batch-select a sprint's worth of issues. Pick your output: release note, changelog, or email. 1 click 3 Review and publish Tune the tone, edit inline, and push straight to Confluence, Intercom, or your changelog page. Drafts stay versioned forever. Ship it See it on a real ticket Below: a raw engineering ticket on the left, and the customer-ready release note Luminos generated on the right — in 2.4 seconds. Raw Jira ticket LUM-2841 summary: fix flaky search; debounce + cache layer type: Bug / Improvement assignee: r.chen desc: search input was firing on every keystroke. added 180ms debounce + LRU cache (n=64). also: ugly minor refactor of useSearch hook. fixes #2841, also kinda fixes #2799. needs QA on safari 16. shipping behind LD flag search-v2-rollout. → Luminos release note Polished Faster, smoother search v3.14 Released for all workspaces · 3 min read Search now feels instant. We've rebuilt the input layer to be smarter about when it queries, so results land the moment you stop typing. Up to 4× fewer requests on long queries Recent searches stay cached for the session Fixes flicker on Safari 16 Rolling out gradually this week — no action needed. 2.4s generation 92% first-draft acceptance 0 source edits Try on your tickets Ready to ship words at code-speed? Join product teams at Stackwire, Driftlane, and Covalent HQ who've replaced "release-notes Friday" with a single keyboard shortcut.
